zh.Scrooby, Nottinghamshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾史盧比
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Brewster於大約1535年出生在英國越可爾多卡斯特時，他的父親William Brewster32歲，母親Maude Mann27歲。他於大約1565年與Mary Smythe在英國諾廷加姆爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1588年被列為master of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth)。他去世於大約1590年在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾史盧比享年57歲。

zh-hans.Scrooby, Nottinghamshire, England: 英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾史盧比
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Brewster于大约1535年出生在英国越可爾多卡斯特时，他的父亲William Brewster32岁，母亲Maude Mann27岁。他于大约1565年与Mary Smythe在英国諾廷加姆爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1588年被列为master of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth)。他去世于大约1590年在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾史盧比享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Brewster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a brewer of beer or ale, from Middle English brewestere, browestere ‘(female) brewer’ (from Old English brēowan ‘to brew’). Brewer is the usual term in southern England, while Brewster is mainly midland, northern, and Scottish.

History: The Mayflower Pilgrim William Brewster (1567–1644) was the son of the bailiff of the manor of Scrooby, Nottinghamshire, home of one of the earliest Puritan congregations. He was a prominent leader in Plymouth Colony from the 1620s until his death.

variants.name: Brewer
meanings: English: occupational name for a brewer of beer or ale, from Middle English brewere ‘brewer’ (an agent derivative of Old English brēowan ‘to brew’). Compare Brewster .
English (of Norman origin): Anglicized form of French Bruyère (see Bruyere ), a habitational name from a place so called in Calvados, France, from Old French bruiere ‘heath’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of Dutch   Brouwer  , German   Brauer   or   Breuer  , etc., all occupational names meaning ‘brewer’.

variants.name: Bruster
meanings: Swiss German: variant of the topographic name Brust 2, the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When William Brewster was born about 1535, in Doncaster, Yorkshire, England, his father, William Brewster, was 32 and his mother, Maude Mann, was 27. He married Mary Smythe about 1565, in Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. In 1588, at the age of 54, his occupation is listed as master of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth). He died about 1590, in Scrooby, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Brewster est né environ 1535, à Doncaster, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William Brewster, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Maude Mann, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Mary Smythe environ 1565, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. En 1588, à l’âge de 54 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est master of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth). Il est décédé environ 1590, à Scrooby, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Brewster um 1535 in Doncaster, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Brewster 32 und seine Mutter Maude Mann 27. Er hat um 1565 in Nottinghamshire, England Mary Smythe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1588, als er 54 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als master of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth) aufgeführt. Er starb um 1590 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Scrooby, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Brewster è nato il circa 1535 nel Doncaster, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, William Brewster, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Maude Mann, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Mary Smythe circa 1565, nel Nottinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1588, all'età di 54 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come master of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth). Morì circa 1590, nel Scrooby, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Brewsterが約1535年にイギリスヨークシュアイアドンカスターで生まれた時，父William Brewsterは32歳，母Maude Mannは27歳であった。約1565年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアでMary Smytheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。1588年，54歳の時，職業はmaster of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth)と記録されている。彼は約1590年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアスクルービーで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Brewster(이)가 대략 1535년 영국 요르크실르 돈카스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Brewster(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Maude Mann(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1565년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 Mary Smythe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1588년, 54세의 나이로 그의 직업은 master of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth)이었다. 그는 대략 1590년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 스크루비에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Brewster nasceu aproximadamente 1535, em Doncaster, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Brewster, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Maude Mann, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Smythe aproximadamente 1565, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1588, com 54 anos, sua profissão aparece como master of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth). Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1590, em Scrooby, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Brewster родился около 1535 г. в местности донкастир, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Brewster было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Maude Mann, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Smythe около 1565 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1588 году, в возрасте 54 лет,  он работал по профессии master of queen's post (appointed by queen elizabeth). Он умер около 1590 г. в местности скруби, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 57 лет.

Murió aproximadamente 1590, en Scrooby, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 57 años.
